title: THE MISSING POINT | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: A warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session.
The topic for the day is the missing point.
Many times we might wonder what is missing?
Why in spite of our efforts individually and collectively to be happy, to experience satisfaction, contentment,
It's missing and we can see it, it's quite obvious if you see the world around us.
We'll cover this in today's session based on Krishna's teachings in the Gita.
Before we start, request all of you to kindly join me.
These prayers are displayed on the screen.
You can kindly also chant them along with me.
And then we will plunge into the discussion.
Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya, Prabhu Nithyananda, Shri Advaita Gadadhara, Shri Vasadi Gaurabhakta Vrinda,
Namo Mahavadanyaya, Krishna Prema Pradayate, Krishnaya Krishna Chaitanya, Namne Gauratvishay Namaha.
Namo Brahmane Devaya, Go Brahmane Hitayacha, Jagadhitaya Krishnaya, Govindaya Namaha.
Namo Brahmane Devaya, Go Brahmane Hitayacha, Jagadhitaya Krishnaya, Govindaya Namaha.
Vanchakalpatarubhyascha, Kripasindhubhyaevacha, Patitanampavnebhyo, Vaishnavebhyo, Namo Namaha.
Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare.
So, today in the discussion on the missing point, we will be broadly covering three important aspects.
Each will have a title and like we have done in our previous sessions, as we move forward,
I would request all of you to kindly mention it in the live chat.
After we have covered each point.
The first point is, they do not know.
I repeat, they do not know.
The one might wonder, who is they and what they do not know.
He said, na te vidhu, vidhu means to know, na te vidhu, swarth gatim hi vishnu, they do not know, they means the entire populace, by and large, majority of the people in this world.
So, na te vidhu, vidhu means to know, swarth gatim hi vishnu, the real swartha, the real self-interest is vishnu, they do not know.
It's quite interesting to observe that each one of us, each one of us, without an exception, we all have different professions, different backgrounds.
Born in different circumstances, different goals, different aspirations.

Each one of us are unique, but still there is one thing in common and that is all of us without an exception, rich man, poor man, Indian, American, educated, uneducated, every one of us without an exception, we are pursuing happiness.
We are wanting to be happy. If we are already happy, we want to increase it. And if we are not happy, we are wanting to be happy.
If we are miserable, we are trying to get out of that miserable condition so that we can be happy.
And as I mentioned, it's not wrong to pursue happiness because that's the quality.
It's the quality of a soul. Anandamayo bhyasat. To be happy. To be happy, to be joyful is not a sin. A soul seeks ananda.
So, but in spite of our desire, our inclination, our aspiration to be happy and to maximize our happiness,
and in spite of we putting a lot of efforts, people are working very hard, people are not lazy to pursue happiness.
There was a time when life was quite relaxed.
People would work for seven to eight hours in their offices, in their workplace.
I have seen that particular life, living in a township, where people would come back from their workplace,
at six o'clock in the evening, 6.30. And then, those were the days when you didn't have, you know, internet,
you didn't have cable television and all that. So, people would invariably have their early dinner by 8, 8.30.
And you will see people strolling, having a night walk, socializing, talking to each other, some people engaging in some sports.
But we don't see that everyone is busy. And we are working very hard.
Gone are the days where a person works six hours, seven hours, eight hours.
These days, 10 hours, 9 hours, 12 hours is very common. We see people working even 14 hours a day.
Now, here, there is a contradiction.
We are seeking happiness. We are pursuing happiness. We are working very hard for it.
Everyone, world over, the scientists, the architects, the builders, the engineers, the doctors,
everyone is working very hard so that we can be happy.
This world can be a wonderful place.
A wonderful place for us to all live.
But statistics, the numbers prove otherwise.
If you see statistics, numbers in terms of the diseases,
if you see in terms of hypertension, blood pressure, anxiety, depression, suicides, abortions,
the numbers speak a different story.
On one side, we want to be happy.
But on the other side, what we are getting, in spite of we working very, very hard, very strenuously to achieve that happiness,
what we are getting in return is something different.
We are more and more unhappy.
We are more and more unhappy.
We are more and more unhappy.
If we could somehow, although we don't have very easy way to measure happiness,
if we could somehow quantify and measure,
just like you can measure temperature, it's very objective, 98.7, 101.2.
If we were to have some measuring scale to measure happiness and if we were to measure happiness
an average person is enjoying or rather is experiencing today in this world,
an average person in this world, what he is experiencing, if we could somehow measure,
some of you may agree with me that man, 25 years back, minus internet, minus all the gadgets,
minus all the modern means of transportation,
all the modern means of transportation and communications,
all the mobile phones, Facebook, social media,
all what we have made for our happiness, communication, transportation,
all that, man, 25 years minus all this, maybe was quite happier, much more happier.
Some of you may agree with this, what I am saying.
So this brings us to a very fundamental question.
Where are we going wrong in our pursuit of happiness?
To be happy is not wrong.
To work hard, we are doing that.
But in spite of working very hard and pursuing happiness,
why is it that we are getting something which is different from what we are pursuing?
Very different from the goal what we have set,
That I want to be happy.
Somebody might want to be happy by becoming an architect.
Somebody might want to be happy by being a chartered accountant.
Somebody might want to be happy to apply for a visa and, you know,
go and settle down in some developed country, be in US, in Europe, some other country.
Details may vary, but then the end objective is the same.
So what is the missing point?
swarth gatim, their real self-interest is Vishnu.
So one time there were three friends.
They were staying on the 30th floor of a building.
And they were sharing a house, an apartment.
So one day after office hours, they came back home.
And they reached the ground floor.
All of them came back together, probably working in the same office.
The lift is under emergency repair.
They are staying on the 30th floor.
And they have come back from the office.
And there they have this notice.
Okay, now we have no option but to take the stairs.
So they all talked to each other and said,
Okay, while we climb the stairs, let's engage ourselves.
Rather than engaging in some meaningless discussion.
Let's recall some anecdote, some story.
And just for a change, let it be something which is tragic.
Let us recall something which we have heard of, some story, some anecdote, which is tragic.
After we reach our home, our apartment on the 30th floor,
Each one will have to tell a story, an anecdote which is tragic.
And we will decide whose story or anecdote was the most tragic.
Let's have this competition and I am sure the time will pass very quickly.
The first time when I heard this story, there were tears in my eyes.
She wanted to have a child.
And unfortunately, every time, be it third month or eighth month,
or sixth month, every time for seven times, they had miscarriages.
The desperate need to have a child.
And eventually, by Lord's grace, it attempt, they begot a child.
And because they had gone through so much of pain, so much of difficulty, so much of anxiety,
they were very, very attached to this child.
They pampered him with everything he wanted.
Right from the day he was born and by the time, this time, as of today, as of present,
So whenever he would go home, go outside for playing or whatever it is,
And one day, while this child had gone in the neighborhood with a small bicycle, small bicycle,
to play and move around with the friends,
and then he stopped and he said,
it's so tragic, it's so tragic.
He says, while he was returning back,
a car hit him from back and he was spot dead.
And when this, I can't explain to you when the mother, the parents heard about this,
what they went through, their whole life collapsed.
I don't think there could be anything more tragic than this.
For a mother who has tried to beget a child,
had a miscarriage seven times, eventually got it,
the only child, and it died untimely because of freak accident.
By the time they had reached the 10th floor,
and then the second one started,
he says, yes, what you have told is quite tragic,
but what I'm going to share with you is even more tragic.
It's, it's, I mean, when I heard this story,
it took me days to reconcile,
to settle down with the shock, with the grief,
which I experienced on hearing this real life story.
And then he went on to explain that there was a family.
And this was, in one sense, a role model.
This family, generally in families, you know, there's always a tiff,
But here it was quite unique.
The husband and wife, they were quite loving, caring.
Never ever they had fought in their life.
the children, very good upbringing, very brilliant,
And one fine day, this whole family had gone on a vacation.
And they had a nice time.
The entire family, they were in a car.
And unfortunately, unfortunately on that day,
the line man, generally, you know, in the railway signals,
wherever the road crosses the tracks,
you have, you know, a line man who closes this thing.
That day, somehow, he missed putting the bridge,
whatever is called, to stop the people to move and cross the track.
They had, they did no mistake.
They were just returning back, normal speed,
They suddenly crossed the track and by that time,
the engine and the train came and they were completely smashed,
The entire family collapsed and died on spot.
The whole bodies were completely run.
By that time, you know, couple of them had started crying.
And by the time, they had almost reached the 25th, 26th floor.
And then, the turn was for the last one to share his story.
And then, he was quite reluctant, quite,
should he share, the story is going to be very tragic.
It will shatter all of them, including the friends who were there.
They will experience the shock of their lives.
So, he was reluctant and they kept climbing.
So, others said, no, no, share, whatever it is.
He says, I have heard both of your stories,
but I think the one I am going to speak is very tragic.
So, they all said, why don't you speak?
And he was reluctant somehow to share this.
He says, somehow, I don't have the courage to share this with you.
I am sure you all will be so shocked, so much dejected hearing this.
And by the time, they had almost reached the 29th floor.
They kept climbing and they reached the 29th floor.
And then, he says, actually, my story is very small.
And then, he said, I am sorry to say,
it is very difficult for you to take it in stride.
I have forgotten the key to our apartment downstairs.
And by the time, these two friends, they were completely, you know, ready to hit him.
They have spent all the time climbing the stairs.
By the time, they reached their apartment, 30th floor.
With great difficulty, climbing the stairs one by one.
And this person is saying that he has forgotten the key.
It should not happen that we also do the same mistake.
And if we are in the same predicament,
Na te vidu svart gatim hi Vishnu.
Then, we will also experience the same thing.
Na te vidu svart gatim hi Vishnu.
The point when we leave this body, leave this world,
All what we have painstakingly worked on,
It is, we have heard it many times.
Kya leke aaye the, kya leke jayenge.
It will be just like, you know, sometimes,
Recently, a boxer from India was, failed a dope test.
Or sometimes, when such things happen,
they are stripped of their memory.
They are stripped of their medals.
Lance Armstrong, you know, he was
I don't know, it is a very rigorous race
And, he was stripped of all his medals.
So, all the achievements will be stripped off.
And, we will feel a sense of dejection.
That whatever I have worked for,
I have to just leave behind everything
So, what is that main thing which,
Na te vidhu svart gatini Vishnu.
that their real self-interest is Vishnu.
We will cover more in detail,
why it is said like that.
and why Vishnu is the real self-interest.
We will cover more in detail.
I request the devotees attending the session today,
to kindly jot down in the live chat,
the heading of the first point.
And, then we move on to the second point.
I am sure some of you might have watched the
But, many people spoke to me,
How do you explain what is being mentioned?
Lot of things were sarcastically portrayed,
and many people were completely shaken,
unless they had good spiritual understanding.
There is a family known to me,
he stopped going to the temple,
He saw in movies certain things,
you know, they are doing Abhisheka,
and that milk going in the drain,
So, you know, he felt that
we all believe and worship statues,
he is not budging from his position,
and he does not want to chant,
does not go to the temple,
does not want to follow any spiritual or religious practice.
So, when I heard so much,
because since we conduct many sessions,
it's important to know what is,
you know, we can address those doubts,
which might have crept in the minds of people.
So, I was speaking to one lady,
this doubt came in my mind also,
she would go to a Shiva temple,
and she would carry a pot of milk,
instead of going to the temple,
and offering that milk to Shivling,
on my way to the temple.
And I could make out that,
and he was asking for food,
gave that milk to that boy,
and as if he was so hungry,
what I saw on his face,
on the face of that child,
and we can see an immediate,
Why should we go to the temple,
we all just have some belief,
and when people do not know,
So, I gave her an example,
We have example of the Durvasa Muni,
we come to the third point,

So, quick recap of what we have covered so far.
The point one was they do not know.
Naate vidu svart gatim hi vishnu.
The second point was watering the root.
If we have to water a plant, although the leaves are drying, we actually put water on the root.
With the understanding, with the knowledge that the water will get distributed everywhere.
And that is the right way of watering a plant.
In fact, if we keep watering the leaves, all the more the water will get, the plant will get dried up.
Because you are not, you are missing the main point.
So, the third point today what we are going to discuss is, please make a note of it.
We all know human life is very valuable.
Out of 84 lakh species of life, 84 lakh yoniya.
After many many life, transmigrating in lower species, we get the human birth.
After many many lives, after many many births, we get this human birth.
And this human birth is very precious.
In this birth, in human form of life, we can find out the missing point.
In no other life form, plants, birds, aquatics, reptiles, animals cannot find out naate vidu,
So the human life is said to be very valuable.
That is why it is asatoma satgamaya.
The sat refers to the missing point what we are talking about today.
So in human form of life we can understand this thing and it is very valuable.
It is said in the Bhagavatam, labdhva sudurlabam bahusambhava ante manusyam janma.
Arthadam means it is very meaningful, very precious.
Only if we know the value then we will try to make the most of this human birth.
But we do not know the value.
Anyone who knows the value, the Bhagavatam, the sages.
Because they know the value, they know the missing point.
Before we again fall into the clutches of death and lose this precious human form.
Toornam means there is an urgency.
Pateta means again we fall into the clutches of death.
Which is very important for our lives.
This is very important for our lives.
This is very important for our family lives.
This is very important for our family lives.

Which is inevitable. Again, we will fall into the clutches of death. Devotee, non-devotee, materialist, atheist, theist, rich man, poor man, black, white, anybody, we will fall into the clutches of death. And all the more we can see in present times.
Devotee, non-devotee, materialist, atheist, rich man, black, white, anybody, we will fall into the clutches of death. And all the more we can see in present times.

These are based on attraction between male and female. And they having material, sensual pleasure in gorgeous ways. We sat even the dogs enjoying that in the street. The setting may be different. They may not be in a cozy apartment, but then the act which gives a pleasure, pleasing sensation is the same.
Agatam is saying before we again fall into the clutches of death, we have a very important responsibility, duty having got this human birth. So that's why the point, the value of the human birth.
Once there was a guru who had a disciple and the disciple put forth a very important question to the guru that everyone knows that human life is precious.
that one day we have to leave this world, then why don't people understand the value?
And why don't they take up spirituality sincerely, seriously?
So, the guru reply probably they do not understand the value. Because they don't have the knowledge.
Because they don't have the knowledge.
So, the student was not quite satisfied with the answer. He could not comprehend what the Guru said, because they do not have the right knowledge. Because based on knowledge, you value things.

So, just to give him a first-hand experience of what he told, the Guru gave him a stone and told, in those days, it was a barter system where you exchange goods. If you want something, you give something and in return, you are given something back.
So, the Guru told him, you go to different, go to marketplace, you try to barter, try to give the stone and try to get something in return.
And let us see how much you are able to get. What do people tell you about this particular stone?
So, first he went to a sweet shop. The owner of the shop and he told him that, can I get that sweet? Here I have a stone, you can take this and in return, can you give me of those sweets?
So, the shop owner mocked at him. He says, get lost, go away, do not waste my time.
After all, how can I, although he did not verbalize, he did not speak, but it was quite obvious from his action, his words, how can a stone be exchanged for sweets?
They are quite costly. They have much more value compared to an ordinary stone, a stone.
So, he was quite dejected with that answer. He was sent back.
And no way he could exchange that stone with the sweets.
So, he went to a, the next place he went was actually a vegetable vendor.

And vegetable vendors, you know, they have that Tharaju, they have that weighing scale, those times, earlier times barter system and they have the weighing scales with, they were not digital scales, they were weighing scales.
We actually have the weights and we have seen people used to weigh with some stones.
So, they somehow have a stone which actually weighs 750 grams or thousand 1 kg.
So, he thought probably this stone might be useful as a weight to measure, but it is a stone.
So, he said, yes, I am willing to take. And in return, I will give you this onion.
And he said, you give me this stone and I will give this onion.
So in other words, his value for that stone was equivalent to value of one onion.
But his guru has said, you show this stone to many people.
He did not give that stone and he decided to go to the next shop to really find out what does that shopkeeper say.
This time he went to a huge jewelry shop.
And right at the entrance, the security guard, first he did not allow, but since he was persistent, somehow he allowed him.
The owner asked him to come in.
And when he went in, so the jeweler, huge shop filled with gold, gold ornaments, jewels, different kinds of precious stones, gems.
So this man showed the stone to the owner.
And he looked at this jeweler and the jeweler's eyes popped out.
And then he folded his hand and he said, where did you get it from?
And then he said, unfortunately, unfortunately, I cannot afford it.
I cannot really, if I were to give everything what I have in my shop, it won't be this thing.
But can I give you some small necklace made of gold just so that I can have a closer look of this stone?
Never ever I have in my life come across this precious stone.
So this stone was not an ordinary stone.
